citations.description: AC4 proving script: `assert d['n_plans']>=14 and set(('fleiss_kappa','raw_agreement','pass'))<=set(d) and d['pass']==(d['fleiss_kappa']>=0.6 and d['raw_agreement']>=0.8)`

findings.criterion: Exp B passes its floor (order-shuffle stability) — `runs/e6_summary.json` must have `order_shuffle.n_plans >= 14`
findings.detail: The proving script asserts `d['n_plans']>=14`, but `e6_summary.json` records `"n_plans": 10`. Four plans (`8722-f153-bd26-46d8`, `8bda-cd4b-3459-46da`, `d015-8af0-b627-4c18`, `e46e-f886-033d-490b`) were excluded at run time because their tickets had been archived/removed from the tickets branch. The summary records `n_plans_total=14` and `n_plans_excluded=4`, and the exclusions are honestly documented, but the `n_plans` field (10) fails the AC's embedded validation assertion `d['n_plans']>=14`. The metric and pass/fail verdict are consistent with the actual data (κ=0.55, raw=0.70, pass=false, R3 blocker recorded), but the experiment delivered only 10/14 of the intended sample.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
